context: Solitary Cystic Lesion √   well-defined lesion with predominant near-water attenuation (0-30 HU) / signal intensity exhibiting negligible enhancement at dynamic imaging √  variability of cystic component accounts for differences in attenuation / signal intensity: (a)  liquids: serous, mucinous, bilious, hemorrhagic, proteinaceous, or mixed fluids (b)  intratumoral necrosis (c)  tissue with a high water content √   septa may contain a range of tissues including epithelium, fibrotic tissue, stroma, neoplastic tissue, inflammatory cells.
